techniques.evidence_quote: Cozy Bear used the stolen credentials of user accounts to access the DNC's computer systems
forensic analysis showed that both Cozy Bear and Fancy Bear used the stolen credentials of user accounts to access the DNC's computer systems

techniques.technique_name: Indicator Removal: Clear Windows Event Logs
techniques.evidence_quote: on or about May 13, 2016, the GRU cleared the event logs from a DNC computer
the GRU intentionally deleted logs and computer files to cover their tracks
collected data from DNC computers, including keylogs and screenshots
sent forged notification emails to Senate staff members, claiming the staff member's Microsoft Exchange password had expired
collected data from DNC computers, including keylogs and screenshots
the GRU hacked into the DNC's email server and stole thousands of emails
unauthorized users had accessed the DNC's cloud-computing service
The attackers targeted the DNC's research department, document repositories
Linux-based version of X-Agent malware, programmed to communicate with the GRU-registered domain linuxkrnl.net
GRU later used proprietary malware known as 'X-Tunnel' to move this data outside of the DNC network through encrypted channels
Russian hackers—including the GRU Operatives—purchased servers in the United States, registered several domain names
GRU then used malware known as 'X-Tunnel' to exfiltrate this stolen DNC data to a GRU-leased computer located in Illinois
unauthorized users had stolen the contents of these virtual servers by making exact duplicates ("snapshots") of them and moving those snapshots to other accounts they owned on the same service
